root_text.an6.73:1.1: “Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharituṁ.
root_text.an6.73:1.4: Kāmesu kho panassa ādīnavo na yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭho hoti.
root_text.an6.73:1.5: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme appahāya abhabbo paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharituṁ.
root_text.an6.73:2.1: Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharituṁ.
root_text.an6.73:2.4: kāmesu kho panassa ādīnavo na yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭho hoti.
root_text.an6.73:2.5: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme pahāya bhabbo paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharitun”ti.

translation_text.an6.73:1.1: “Mendicants, without giving up these six qualities you can’t enter and remain in the first absorption.
translation_text.an6.73:1.3: Desire for sensual pleasures, ill will, dullness and drowsiness, restlessness and remorse, and doubt.
translation_text.an6.73:1.4: And the drawbacks of sensual pleasures have not been truly seen clearly with right wisdom.
translation_text.an6.73:1.5: Without giving up these six qualities you can’t enter and remain in the first absorption.
translation_text.an6.73:2.1: But after giving up these six qualities you can enter and remain in the first absorption.
translation_text.an6.73:2.3: Desire for sensual pleasures, ill will, dullness and drowsiness, restlessness and remorse, and doubt.
translation_text.an6.73:2.4: And the drawbacks of sensual pleasures have been truly seen clearly with right wisdom.
translation_text.an6.73:2.5: After giving up these six qualities you can enter and remain in the first absorption.”
